title: TC001-Complete onboarding successfully and land on Tasks
description: Verifies a logged-in user can complete the onboarding flow and is redirected to Tasks at the end.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/onboarding' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Step 1').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Login form not found on /login: page contains 0 interactive elements
- SPA did not render on http://localhost:5173/login, preventing completion of the onboarding flow

title: TC003-Onboarding completion redirects user to Tasks
description: Verifies that after completing onboarding, the user is redirected to the Tasks page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (use explicit navigate to http://localhost:5173/login as the test step requires).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Fill the email (index 219) and password (index 230) fields, then click the Log in button (index 241).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('pedrogoulart9036@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Pady5s0!')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/onboarding' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Onboarding complete').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/tasks' in frame.url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Login did not redirect to '/onboarding' after submitting valid credentials.
- Current page shows 0 interactive elements (blank UI), so the onboarding UI is not reachable.
- Finish button not found on the page; onboarding cannot be completed.
- 'Onboarding complete' text and the '/tasks' URL could not be verified because the onboarding step never loaded.

        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/div/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Maverick')
        
        # -> Dismiss the onboarding modal by filling the name and nickname fields and clicking the onboarding action button so the finances UI (including 'Add Transaction') becomes accessible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/div/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Test User')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Maverick')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Continuar' button (index 2266) to dismiss the onboarding modal so the finances UI (including the 'Add Transaction' control) becomes accessible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/div[2]/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the onboarding 'Continuar' button (index=2676) to dismiss the onboarding modal so the finances UI (including 'Add Transaction') becomes accessible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/div[2]/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC020-AI category suggestion fails and user saves transaction with manual category
description: Verifies the UI shows an error toast on suggestion failure and allows manual category selection before saving.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (use explicit navigate to http://localhost:5173/login as the test step requires).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the provided email into the email field (index 205) and the provided password into the password field (index 204), then click the Sign in button (index 208) and wait for the app to load.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('pedrogoulart9036@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('Pady5s0!')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Finanças' sidebar link to open the finances page so the transaction flow can be tested.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[3]/aside/div/aside/nav/a[6]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Pular' (Skip) button to dismiss the onboarding modal so the finances page UI (including Add Transaction) can be accessed.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/div[2]/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/finances' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Category suggestion failed').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=AI fail path test').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

- Finances page failed to load: a runtime error 'Failed to fetch dynamically imported module: http://localhost:5173/src/features/finances/index.tsx' is displayed.
